title: Blend a recurrent discrete-time hazard model
summary: Pit stops are recurrent time-to-event decisions within stints, not independent row labels.
rationale: Pit stops are recurrent time-to-event decisions within stints, not independent row labels. Discrete-time survival models directly estimate conditional event hazard given no event yet, matching PitNextLap better than a generic classifier and complementing tree ensembles.
implementation_hint: Build a sidecar model on driver-race stint risk sets with baseline hazards by TyreLife, LapNumber, RaceProgress bin, Stint, PriorPitCount, Compound, and rule debt. Use logistic/cloglog regression, GAM/EBM, or shallow LightGBM, then OOF-blend its hazard probabilities with the AutoGluon predictor.
expected_effect: Likely modest AUC gain from better ordering around early-stint zeros, final-window spikes, and repeat-stop cooldowns.
risk: If the main GBDT already learns the hazard shape, the blend may add little; censoring and reset logic must be fold-safe.
